猜想.摘要: Best-available closure keeps 'neutral aromatic' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: The main gap is still pair-specific discrimination between neutral aromatic and TICT, now centered on missing external supplement after the improved internal conformer-bundle parse still failed to yield torsion/planarity descriptors.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.62) and top2 is TICT (0.15), with ICT third (0.13). The latest evidence modestly strengthens the current top1 because the targeted microscopic parse of the new conformer bundle still failed to reveal the torsion/planarity observables that would positively support a twisting-dominant interpretation; instead, the overall internal picture remains more consistent with a rigid neutral aromatic scaffold. The same evidence weakens the top2 challenger TICT slightly: TICT stays structurally plausible because the molecule is rotor-rich, but the best new internal pairwise test still did not produce positive twisting-sensitive support. ICT is also slightly de-emphasized because the latest evidence is geometry-focused and still does not supply any CT-positive signal. No still-credible additional hypothesis remains in coverage debt; portfolio screening is legitimately complete, so the case remains in pairwise contraction. The key remaining discriminator is now external pair-specific evidence for neutral aromatic versus TICT, because the direct internal geometry-parse route has again hit the same missing-deliverable ceiling even on the newer artifact set. The chosen next action is verifier because, after this changed internal observable has still failed to supply pair-discriminative torsion evidence, external supplementation is now the highest-information next step.

Best-available closure keeps 'neutral aromatic' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: The main gap is still pair-specific discrimination between neutral aromatic and TICT, now centered on missing external supplement after the improved internal conformer-bundle parse still failed to yield torsion/planarity descriptors.

External supplementation for neutral aromatic_vs_TICT remains missing because previous verifier attempts failed operationally before returning usable evidence cards. After the latest internal pairwise parse also hit a deliverable ceiling, verifier evidence is again the most informative next step.

Internal evidence favors neutral aromatic and has now exhausted the most natural low-cost geometry parse route on the new conformer bundle, but closure is still not sufficient because the active pair lacks successful external supplementation.

Portfolio screening remains complete in round 9 of 20 with 12 rounds remaining. The latest microscopic parse did not reopen any coverage debt. All credible alternatives remain either directly screened, capability-blocked, or dropped with reason, so the case legitimately remains in pairwise contraction.

Macro screening has already extracted the most useful deterministic structural proxies, and microscopic artifact parsing has now twice exposed a concrete ceiling: the available artifact parses do not return the torsion/planarity deliverables needed for a stronger internal TICT test. The verifier route remains potentially high-value, but prior failures were operational rather than scientific, so a more tightly bounded retry is justified. Recent working memory repeats the same verifier local uncertainty, so repeating the same verifier route is unlikely to shrink the current gap: Verifier evidence cards summarize external supervision but do not replace Planner-level synthesis; unresolved local gap: the evidence cards still need Planner-level synthesis before any mechanism decision.

Latest microscopic evidence: extract_geometry_descriptors_from_bundle successfully parsed the new round_08_conformer_bundle without new calculations and returned 3 parsed records, but the key pair-discriminative deliverables remained missing: torsion and planarity. No proton-transfer-type motif was found. This is interpreted as absence of new positive TICT support rather than decisive exclusion.
